website_note: Active site confirmed; company name identified from website as Crider & Crider, Inc., Bloomington, IN
description: Family-owned and operated civil and highway contractor based in Bloomington, Indiana, founded in 1954 and incorporated in 1979, performing projects in heavy/highway and commercial building construction including utilities, sewer/water, site development, subdivisions, and athletic fields.

Original entry had null company name and state — identified as Crider & Crider, Inc., Bloomington, IN. BBB verified at 1900 Liberty Dr, Bloomington, IN 47403.

Contact Steven Crider confirmed as owner/secretary. No additional senior leadership names publicly listed.

website_note: Active site confirmed at pheifer.com; company is based in Neenah, WI
description: Third-generation family-owned bridge construction company based in Neenah, Wisconsin, founded in 1952 by brothers Robert and Alan Pheifer, now led by Ryan Pheifer as sole owner and President, specializing in bridge construction throughout Wisconsin for the Wisconsin DOT and other clients.
recent_news: Awarded $846,790 contract in May 2024 by Governor Tony Evers for rehabilitation of the bridge carrying WIS 35 over the Big River in Pierce County. Also active bidder on multiple WisDOT projects in 2024-2026, including a 2026 award for the T Linn – Hillside Road Bridge in Walworth County.
verification_notes: Tyler Volkmann listed as Project Manager — could not independently verify this contact in public sources beyond secondary data aggregators. Ryan Pheifer confirmed as President/Owner from LinkedIn and company website. Founded year discrepancy: batch says 1950, verified sources say 1952.
enrichment_notes: Company is an active Wisconsin DOT contractor specializing in bridge construction. Founded year corrected from 1950 (batch) to 1952 (company website and multiple verified sources). Third-generation family ownership under Ryan Pheifer. WisDOT Structure of the Year award recipient.

company_name: WAGNER HIGH SCHOOL (KAREN WAGNER HIGH SCHOOL / JUDSON ISD)
website_note: Active site confirmed; direct school URL is wagner.judsonisd.org; school is part of Judson ISD in unincorporated Bexar County, TX
description: Karen J. Wagner High School is a public secondary school operated by Judson Independent School District, located in unincorporated Bexar County, Texas, serving approximately 2,218 students as of 2023-24, named after Lt. Col. Karen J. Wagner who died at the Pentagon on September 11, 2001.
recent_news: School continues annual solemn ceremonies honoring its namesake Lt. Col. Karen J. Wagner. Judson ISD Superintendent Dr. Milton R. Fields III leads the district; school had 2,218 students in 2023-24.
verification_notes: Lauren Bush as a Teacher is plausible but could not be confirmed in public sources. Principal Blas Arce and Superintendent Dr. Fields confirmed from district website and news sources. Founding year 2017 consistent with school opening date.
enrichment_notes: This is a public high school, not a private entity. The website judsonisd.org is the district site; the school-specific URL is wagner.judsonisd.org. Employee count of 14 and revenue of $26.4M appear to be template/placeholder values for a school with 2,200+ students.

website_note: Parent organization American Baptist Homes of the Midwest at abhomes.net; primary community website is elmcrest.net. Located at 2104 12th St, Harlan, IA 51537.

Christenson may be a former administrator. Industry listed in source data as 'Credit Granting' — this is incorrect; actual industry is senior living/retirement.
enrichment_notes: Source data listed industry as 'Credit Granting' which is a data error — this is a senior living community. Founding year estimated as approximately 1959 based on '65+ years' claim on their website. CMS 5-Star rated facility. Parent org ABHM founded 1930 and nonprofit.

website_note: Active site confirmed at bataviacsd.org; district headquartered in Batavia, NY (Genesee County). 2024-25 budget of $60.3M far exceeds $26.4M listed in source data.
description: Public K-12 school district in Batavia, New York serving approximately 2,200 students across 4 schools: Batavia High School, Batavia Middle School, John Kennedy Intermediate, and Jackson Primary School. The district has 30% minority enrollment and 41.3% economically disadvantaged students.

contacts.name: Joel Reed
contacts.title: Director of Health, Physical Education and Athletics

recent_news: July 2024: Board of Education approved contract extension for Superintendent Jason Smith through 2029. July 2024: District named new Middle School Principal and Director of Health/PE/Athletics (Dr. Megan Crine and Joel Reed). $45M capital improvement project approved by voters in December 2023, design submitted, construction expected to begin spring 2025. 2024-25 budget of $60,294,755 approved by voters.
verification_notes: Original contact name was null in source data; no original contact to verify. Superintendent Jason Smith identified and confirmed from multiple sources. Revenue figure of $26.4M in source data is significantly understated; actual annual budget is $60.3M. Employee count of 13 is also understated for a district with 2,200+ students.
enrichment_notes: Revenue ($26.4M) and employee count (13) in source data are clearly placeholder/template values for a school district with a $60.3M budget. Jason Smith confirmed as Superintendent, contract extended through 2029. A state audit previously noted hundreds of missing devices. No original contacts to verify.

company_name: COUNTY OF JOHNSON (JOHNSON COUNTY, KY — PVA OFFICE)
website_note: Active site confirmed at johnsoncountypva.com; this is the PVA (Property Valuation Administrator) office for Johnson County, KY located in Paintsville, KY 41240
description: Johnson County, Kentucky Property Valuation Administrator (PVA) office, responsible for assessing property values for tax purposes throughout Johnson County. The PVA is an elected official position separate from the County Judge Executive. Johnson County was established in 1843 and is headquartered in Paintsville, KY.

contacts.name: Mr. Mark McKenzie
contacts.title: County Judge Executive (NOT PVA — different position)

recent_news: 2024: State auditor released Agreed-Upon Procedures (AUP) report for Johnson County PVA for period July 1, 2023 – June 30, 2024, identifying exceptions including lack of receipts/disbursements ledger and failure to conduct monthly bank reconciliations. Ray E. Davis III announced 2024 candidacy for Johnson County PVA position.
verification_notes: Mark McKenzie is confirmed as County Judge Executive (chief executive of the county government), not the Property Valuation Administrator. The PVA is a separate elected official — currently Michael 'Dip' Stafford. The source data appears to have assigned the Judge Executive to a PVA-related entry. Industry listed as 'Security' is incorrect — this is a government/property valuation entity.
enrichment_notes: Source data lists industry as 'Security' which is incorrect — this is Johnson County, KY government PVA office. Mark McKenzie as County Judge Executive is the county's chief executive, but he is separate from the PVA which is held by Michael Stafford. State audit in 2024 found bookkeeping deficiencies. County established in 1843.

website_note: Active site confirmed; co.johnson.in.us redirects to johnsoncounty.in.gov — the official Johnson County, Indiana government website. County seat is Franklin, IN.
description: Johnson County, Indiana county government established in 1822, serving a growing suburban county south of Indianapolis. The Board of County Commissioners (executive body) oversees county buildings, lands, roads, bridges, and county departments, with responsibility for authorizing contracts and supervising many county functions.

contacts.name: Brian Baird
contacts.title: County Commissioner, District 1 (2025 Chairman; also 2025 Indiana County Commissioners President)

recent_news: December 2024: Commissioner Brian Baird named 2025 President of the statewide Indiana County Commissioners organization. Johnson County officials won awards at the Indiana County Commissioners conference. $11M behavioral health center annex (next to Johnson Memorial Health, Franklin campus) anticipated to open July 2025, funded with $10M from ARPA funds.
verification_notes: Source data had null company name; identified as Johnson County, Indiana from website co.johnson.in.us. No original contact to verify. Three commissioners (Baird, Walls, West) confirmed from official county website. Note: this is a different entity from entry #19 (Johnson County, KY PVA office).
enrichment_notes: Source data had null company name, null state, null industry. Identified as Johnson County, Indiana county government from co.johnson.in.us website. County established 1822. Brian Baird named 2025 Indiana County Commissioners President in December 2024 — a significant recognition. $11M behavioral health project underway with ARPA funding.
